page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.mn4.xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the ecs.mn4 family. It is configured with 4 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical Intel Xeon 8269CY cores running at 2.5 GHz with a shared CPU allocation. The instance features 16.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB per core, and lacks local storage and GPU hardware. Network connectivity is limited to a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place this instance primarily in the lower tiers. In CPU-centric evaluations, including stress-ng and Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests, the server scores in the bottom 25%. Cryptographic and compression tasks show similar constraints, with single-threaded gzip and bzip3 decompression falling into the bottom 10%. Memory write bandwidth for large blocks is a relative bright spot, performing in the middle 50%, whereas read bandwidth remains in the bottom 25%.

Given the shared CPU allocation and weak overall benchmark results, the ecs.mn4.xlarge is best suited for non-critical, low-intensity workloads. These include basic web serving, light database operations, and development environments that do not require sustained high CPU performance. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the instance offers a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio suitable for budget-conscious general-purpose applications where performance consistency is not a primary requirement.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.mn4.xlarge is a general-purpose server instance featuring 4 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz on an x86_64 architecture. It operates under a shared CPU allocation model and does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Network performance is defined by a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmark results consistently position this instance in the lower performance tiers, with CPU, memory read, and compression tests largely scoring in the bottom 25% or bottom 10%. However, memory write bandwidth for large blocks achieves average performance. Due to these performance characteristics and its shared resource model, the ecs.mn4.xlarge is best suited for low-demand workloads such as basic web serving, development environments, and lightweight database operations where cost efficiency is prioritized over high-performance guarantees.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.mn4.xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 4 shared vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8269CY CPU. It is suited for low-intensity workloads and budget-conscious environments.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.mn4.xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 4 shared vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 1 Gbps network baseline, ideal for light workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly general-purpose instance with shared CPU resources and balanced memory for light workloads and development environments.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8269CY processor with 4 vCPUs and 16.0 GB RAM
Shared CPU allocation model on KVM hypervisor
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Average performance in large-block memory write benchmarks
Best suited for lightweight web serving and development environments
